reviews.review_title: Some potential , but not there yet
reviews.review_description:
When I first got this game, I was unable to play it and left a review to that effect. Original review is below. The developer responded immediately with some standard advice about getting hand tracking to work. I had already tried all of their suggestions and I was still stuck at the opening screen with the message telling me to enable hand tracking.  I noticed the game is designed to be played in a standing position. I had been trying to launch the game while sitting . So I tried again by standing , launching the app with my hand (no controllers), and holding my hands in front of my headset.  This combination worked . I was in. As to the gameplay , it’s very one note. You punch space rocks. They don’t break apart like the original Asteroids game into smaller pieces to destroy.  Just punch or karate chop and they disappear. A brief shower of pebbles might be better . You can also collect gems, but they don’t seem to have any purpose. If you don’t hit the rocks, they hit you in the head with no repercussions . How about a health meter that goes down ? A couple of things I like include the cool blue-star pattern on your hands and the way the rocks spin and bounce when you knock them into each other. Realistic. There just needs to be a lot more to this game, with more stuff to punch, more variety , maybe a goal to reach for each level. A funny alien craft shooting at you so you have to dodge it. No need to muck it up with a story or anything. Simple is good , but this game has a ways to go before it would bring you back to play another day. I do appreciate the developer’s quick response. I look forward to new features . I sure did love that old Atari Asteroids.

Original Review: Unfortunately, I’m unable to play this game at all. Can’t get past a message saying hand tracking isn’t enabled. Well, it is. I double checked and am using it with other games. There also was no option that I could see to continue to play with the controllers instead.

developer_response.body:
Hi RogueWoman, thanks for sharing your experience. I’m sorry to hear you’re having trouble getting the game started. I understand it’s frustrating when hand tracking isn’t working as expected.

*Since the game requires your hands to be shown in front of you for tracking to work, here are a few things to check:

*Hand Position: Make sure your hands are within the tracking area, positioned directly in front of you. If they’re slightly off to the side or too far away, the system might not detect them properly. Try keeping your hands centered and within a clear line of sight.

*Lighting and Room Setup: Hand tracking works best in a well-lit environment where the cameras can clearly see your hands. Make sure there’s enough light, and that your hands are visible within the tracking area.

*Hand Speed and Movements: If your hands are moving too quickly, it can be harder for the system to track them. Try moving more slowly or holding your hands steady for a moment to allow the system to detect them properly.

*Try Restarting the Game: Occasionally, restarting the game can resolve issues where hand tracking isn’t recognized, especially after updates or changes in settings.

If these steps don’t resolve the issue, please feel free to reach out with more details, and we’ll help troubleshoot further. We appreciate your feedback and are actively working on making the game as smooth as possible for everyone!

Thanks again, and I hope you can get back into the game soon!
